title: Udyam Registration (MSME Certificate)
tagline: India's MSME certificate — unlocks collateral-free credit, government procurement priority, and 20+ central schemes.
unlocks: CGTMSE: Collateral-free loans up to ₹5 crore from 100+ banks/NBFCs
NSIC Single Point Registration for government tenders
Priority Sector Lending: banks must lend 7.5% of ANBC to MSMEs
Interest subvention of 2% on MSME loans (government pays part of your interest)
Protection under MSME Development Act: buyers must pay within 45 days

steps.title: Check MSME Category
steps.description: Determine whether you qualify as Micro, Small, or Medium enterprise based on investment and turnover.
details: MICRO: Investment ≤ ₹1 crore AND Turnover ≤ ₹5 crore
SMALL: Investment ≤ ₹10 crore AND Turnover ≤ ₹50 crore
MEDIUM: Investment ≤ ₹50 crore AND Turnover ≤ ₹250 crore
Investment: Plant, machinery, equipment (not land, buildings)
Turnover: As per GST filing or Income Tax return
Early-stage startups with minimal investment and revenue qualify as MICRO
tips: Even startups with zero revenue can register — just declare ₹0 investment and ₹0 turnover
Self-declaration — no documents need to be uploaded or submitted

steps.title: Go to Udyam Registration Portal
steps.description: Visit the official Udyam portal — no login account needed, it's Aadhaar OTP-based.
details: Go to udyamregistration.gov.in
Click 'For New Entrepreneurs who are not Registered yet as MSME'
You'll need the Aadhaar number of the proprietor/managing partner/authorised signatory
The Aadhaar must be linked to a mobile number for OTP
tips: The portal works best on Chrome or Firefox
If Aadhaar is not linked to mobile, visit nearest Aadhaar enrollment center first (30 minutes, free)

steps.title: Enter Aadhaar Details
steps.description: Enter Aadhaar number of the business owner and verify via OTP.
details: Enter 12-digit Aadhaar number of proprietor / managing partner / CEO
Enter name exactly as on Aadhaar card
Click 'Validate and Generate OTP'
Enter OTP received on Aadhaar-linked mobile number
Name will auto-populate from UIDAI — verify it's correct
tips: If OTP fails 3 times, wait 30 minutes before trying again

steps.title: Enter PAN and Business Details
steps.description: Provide enterprise PAN and basic business information.
details: Enter Enterprise PAN (business entity's PAN, not personal PAN for sole proprietors — they're the same)
Select type of organisation: Proprietorship / Partnership / Hindu Undivided Family / LLP / Company / Others
Enter enterprise name (as registered with MCA or trade name)
Social category of promoter: General / SC / ST / OBC
Gender of entrepreneur (for classification statistics)
Specially-abled: Yes/No
Email and mobile number (can be different from Aadhaar-linked)
Official address with PIN code
tips: If you're a Private Limited Company, use the company's CIN-linked PAN
Social category matters for SC-ST Hub access and women entrepreneur scheme prioritization

steps.title: Enter Activity and Investment Details
steps.description: Specify your business activity and declare investment and turnover figures.
details: Main activity: Manufacturing OR Service (select your primary activity)
NIC code: 5-digit National Industrial Classification code for your activity (search NIC code list online)
Number of employees: current headcount (all types — permanent, contract, part-time)
Investment in Plant and Machinery / Equipment (in ₹ lakhs) — enter ₹0 for digital/software startups
Turnover for last financial year (in ₹ lakhs) — enter ₹0 if pre-revenue
Bank account details: IFSC code and account number of business account
documents_needed: Bank passbook / cheque (for IFSC and account number)
tips: For SaaS/software startups: NIC code 62011 (Computer programming activities) or 63990 (Other information service activities)
For EdTech: NIC code 85499 (Other education not elsewhere classified)
Investment in equipment includes computers, servers, phones — not office furniture

steps.title: Submit and Download Certificate
steps.description: Submit the self-declaration and immediately download your Udyam Registration Certificate.
details: Check the declaration checkbox: 'I hereby declare...' (certifying accuracy)
Click 'Submit' — an OTP will be sent to the mobile/email provided
Enter OTP and click 'Final Submit'
Your Udyam Registration Certificate is generated IMMEDIATELY
Download the PDF certificate — contains your Udyam Registration Number (URN): UAM-XX-XX-XXXXXXX
Certificate is also emailed to you
tips: Save the certificate PDF permanently and share with your bank, insurance company, and scheme applications
Udyam certificate does not expire — valid until your enterprise crosses Medium limit (₹250 crore turnover)
Print a copy for your office — many government offices ask for physical copy

steps.title: Apply for CGTMSE Credit Guarantee
steps.description: With Udyam certificate, apply for collateral-free credit guarantee at your bank.
details: Visit your business current account bank (SBI, HDFC, ICICI, Axis — all CGTMSE members)
Apply for business loan with Udyam certificate + DPIIT number (if applicable)
Request the bank to cover the loan under CGTMSE guarantee scheme
CGTMSE provides 75-85% guarantee cover — reduces bank's risk, enables collateral-free lending
Loan limit: up to ₹5 crore under CGTMSE 3.0
Interest rate: typically 10-14% for MSME loans under CGTMSE
tips: SBI and Bank of Baroda are most MSME-friendly for CGTMSE loans
Public sector banks mandatory process CGTMSE — private banks have more flexibility but are harder to navigate
SIDBI also provides direct MSME loans — apply at sidbi.in if bank is unresponsive

faqs.q: Can a Private Limited Company register as MSME?
faqs.a: Yes! All legal entity types — Private Limited, LLP, Partnership, Sole Proprietorship, HUF — can register as MSMEs under Udyam.

faqs.q: Should I get both DPIIT and Udyam registrations?
faqs.a: YES — get both. They unlock different benefits. DPIIT for startup-specific schemes (tax exemption, seed fund). Udyam for MSME schemes (CGTMSE credit, NSIC tenders). They're complementary, not substitutes.

faqs.q: What if my turnover exceeds ₹5 crore — do I lose MSME status?
faqs.a: No — once registered as Micro, you get 3 years of grace period before being reclassified. This is the '3-year lock-in' protection for growing MSMEs.
Yes, 100% free on the official portal. Ignore agents/lawyers charging ₹1,000-5,000 — it takes 15 minutes yourself.
